children.heading: Transfer of acreage base history
children.label: a Transfer of acreage base history

children.heading: Transfer authorized
children.label: 1 Transfer authorized
children.text: For the purpose of establishing proportionate shares for sugarcane farms under , the Secretary, on application of any producer, with the written consent of all owners of a farm, may transfer the acreage base history of the farm to any other parcels of land of the applicant.section 1359ff(c) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Sugarcane acreage base established under that has been or is converted to nonagricultural use on or after , may be transferred to other land suitable for the production of sugarcane that can be delivered to a processor in a proportionate share State in accordance with this paragraph.section 1359ff(c) of this titleMay 13, 2002

children.heading: Notification
children.label: B Notification
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the Secretary becomes aware of a conversion of any sugarcane acreage base to a nonagricultural use, the Secretary shall notify the 1 or more affected landowners of the transferability of the applicable sugarcane acreage base.

children.heading: Initial transfer period
children.label: C Initial transfer period
children.text: The owner of the base attributable to the acreage at the time of the conversion shall be afforded 90 days from the date of the receipt of the notification under subparagraph (B) to transfer the base to 1 or more farms owned by the owner.

children.label: ii
children.text: have 90 days from the date of the receipt of the notification to transfer the base to 1 or more farms operated by the grower.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If transfers under subparagraphs (B) and (C) cannot be accomplished during the periods specified in those subparagraphs, the county committee of the Farm Service Agency for the applicable county shall place the acreage base in a pool for possible assignment to other farms.

children.heading: Acceptance of requests
children.label: ii Acceptance of requests
children.text: After providing reasonable notice to farm owners, operators, and growers of record in the county, the county committee shall accept requests from owners, operators, and growers of record in the county.

children.heading: Assignment
children.label: iii Assignment
children.text: The county committee shall assign the acreage base to other farms in the county that are eligible and capable of accepting the acreage base, based on a random drawing from among the requests received under clause (ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any acreage base remaining unassigned after the transfers and processes described in subparagraphs (A) through (E) shall be made available to the State committee of the Farm Service Agency for allocation among the remaining county committees in the State representing counties with farms eligible for assignment of the base, based on a random drawing.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: ii Allocation
children.text: Any county committee receiving acreage base under this subparagraph shall allocate the acreage base to eligible farms using the process described in subparagraph (E).

children.label: ii
children.text: be subject to the transfer provisions of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Preservation of acreage base history
children.label: b Preservation of acreage base history
children.text: If for reasons beyond the control of a producer on a farm, the producer is unable to harvest an acreage of sugarcane for sugar or seed with respect to all or a portion of the proportionate share established for the farm under , the Secretary, on the application of the producer and with the written consent of all owners of the farm, may preserve for a period of not more than 5 consecutive years the acreage base history of the farm to the extent of the proportionate share involved. The Secretary may permit the proportionate share to be redistributed to other farms, but no acreage base history for purposes of establishing acreage bases shall accrue to the other farms by virtue of the redistribution of the proportionate share.section 1359ff(c) of this title

children.heading: Revisions of allocations and proportionate shares
children.label: c Revisions of allocations and proportionate shares
children.text: The Secretary, after such notice as the Secretary by regulation may prescribe, may revise or amend any allocation of a marketing allotment under , or any proportionate share established or adjusted for a farm under , on the same basis as the initial allocation or proportionate share was required to be established.section 1359dd of this titlesection 1359ff(c) of this title

children.heading: Transfer authorized
children.label: 1 Transfer authorized
children.text: A producer in a proportionate share State, upon written consent from all affected crop-share owners (or the representative of the affected crop-share owners) of a farm may deliver sugarcane to another processing company if the additional delivery, when combined with such other processing company’s existing deliveries, does not exceed the processing capacity of the company.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of acres of sugarcane base being transferred; and

children.label: B
children.text: the pro rata amount of allocation at the processing company holding the applicable allocation that equals the contribution of the grower to allocation of the processing company for the sugarcane acreage base being transferred.
